## CRITICAL — Act Today

### 1. H&M Group (SE) — Named Buyer Identified: Adam Ull

**Signal type:** Named buyer confirmed
**Who:** Adam Ull — Global Product Information Lead at H&M Group
**Source:** eCommerce Expo 2026 speaker list + Retail Technology Innovation Hub coverage
**LinkedIn:** https://se.linkedin.com/in/adamull

**What he said publicly:**
> "The quantity of data is growing, so we need to focus on improving the quality of data in order to offer value."

At eCommerce Expo, Ull delivered a keynote titled *"The Future of Commerce at H&M"* focused on Product Information Management (PIM), the H&M Product Data Hub (PDH), and distributing product data across global digital channels. He uses Bluestone PIM.

**Why it matters:**
This is the exact buyer JAKALA needs at H&M. He has publicly confirmed the problem (product data quality), the investment (PDH), and the gap (quality vs. quantity). His language maps directly to the Data Revenue Diagnostic entry offer. The cap rule is now removed — H&M can move from 25% → ~65% win probability.

**Forecast impact:** +€450K probability-weighted value (€900K × 65% vs. €900K × 25% previously)

**GTM match:** Data Revenue Unlock → Speedtrain
**Entry offer:** Data Revenue Diagnostic — lead with product data quality framing. Reference the PDH and his eCommerce Expo keynote directly.
**Outreach angle:** "You said quality, not quantity — our diagnostic maps exactly where the gap is between the data you have and the revenue it should be driving."
**Suggested action:** Connect on LinkedIn today. Reference eCommerce Expo keynote. Do not pitch — ask a genuine question about the PDH architecture.
**Estimated deal:** €900K
**Window:** His public profile and conference appearances make this a warm outreach, not cold.

## URGENT — Act This Week

### 2. Matas Group (DK) — New Group CEO with AI/Loyalty Background

**Signal type:** Executive transition — buying window opens
**Who:** Mette Uglebjerg — new Group CEO, joining April 15, 2026
**Previous role:** SVP, Alimentation Couche-Tard — Global Food & Marketing (North America, Europe, Asia)
**Couche-Tard mandate included:** Digital development, loyalty programs, AI for efficiency and customer relevance.

**Why it matters:**
Matas currently has no named buyer (capped at 25% win probability). Mette Uglebjerg changes everything. She joins in 33 days with an explicit mandate to drive "the next phase of the Nordic growth journey" and a track record of deploying AI and loyalty data in large retail operations. Her first 90 days = agenda-setting window. The strategic direction is "Win the Nordics" — multi-market expansion + customer experience leadership.

This is a CEO-level buyer with a board mandate to invest in exactly what JAKALA delivers.

**GTM match:** AI Readiness Accelerator → Data Revenue Unlock
**Entry offer:** AI Readiness Diagnostic — "what comes after SymphonyAI category management" framing, plus Nordic multi-market personalization angle.
**Outreach angle:** Lead with her Couche-Tard AI/loyalty background. Frame JAKALA's diagnostic as how Nordic retailers at Matas's scale activate loyalty data across markets.
**Named buyer:** Mette Uglebjerg (CEO, from April 15, 2026)
**Secondary:** Thomas Grane (Director of IT — existing contact point for technical conversations)
**Timing:** Reach out in the week of April 14–18, 2026 — first week in role.
**Pre-work now:** Prepare Matas-specific briefing, outreach draft, and account narrative before April 15.
**Estimated deal:** €700K
**Forecast impact:** Matas moves from 25% capped → 40–55% once Uglebjerg is in seat and outreach is sent.

## STRATEGIC WARNING — Trumf / NorgesGruppen

**Signal type:** Competitive intelligence — incumbent vendor with equity stake
**Competitor:** Forte Digital
**Relationship:** Forte Digital is the strategic app and platform partner for Trumf and NorgesGruppen. **NorgesGruppen owns 10% of Forte Digital.** Forte built the Trumf app, Trumf Pay, and loyalty UX. This is a co-ownership relationship, not just a vendor contract.

**Why it matters:**
JAKALA cannot and should not compete with Forte Digital at the loyalty platform or app layer for Trumf. Forte is embedded, co-owned, and entrenched. Positioning JAKALA as a platform or loyalty app partner will fail immediately.

**What this means for the Trumf GTM:**
The entry point must be explicitly *adjacent* to Forte's domain — not competing:
- Forte owns: loyalty app, Trumf Pay, digital UX, Trumf platform development
- **JAKALA owns the gap:** retail media monetization, data revenue strategy, personalization intelligence above the app layer, and the commercial activation of the loyalty data Forte collects

**Revised outreach angle for Rikke Etholm-Idsøe:**
Do NOT frame around "loyalty platform" or "app improvement." Frame 100% around: *"You have 3M+ members and a new Commercial Director role — our diagnostic maps where that data should be generating retail media and personalization revenue that isn't flowing today."*

**Action:** Update Trumf outreach draft to remove any platform/app language. Focus on retail media revenue activation only.

## HIGH — Act This Month

### 3. Helly Hansen (NO) — Kontoor Integration Accelerating, Investor Day Oslo September 2026

**Signal type:** Post-acquisition integration — digital modernization window
**What happened:** Kontoor Brands (acquired Helly Hansen June 2025 for ~$957M) has announced a **Helly Hansen Investor Day on September 2, 2026 in Oslo**, with 9% revenue growth target for FY2026. Integration is accelerating — over $40M in synergies identified in sourcing, logistics, and distribution.

**Why it matters:**
Post-acquisition integration at this scale creates digital commerce disruption. Helly Hansen currently runs 55 Adobe Commerce sites with 65,000 SKUs per site — a legacy multi-site architecture that Kontoor will need to rationalize as they drive cost synergies and brand coherence. The Investor Day in Oslo signals Helly Hansen is being positioned as a growth asset, which means digital investment to match. The CDO/Head of Digital will be under pressure to modernize before the Investor Day showcases the brand.

**GTM match:** Commerce Optimization → Shopify Commerce Build
**Entry offer:** Commerce Optimization Pilot — multi-site rationalization and PIM architecture review ahead of Kontoor integration demands.
**Outreach angle:** "55 Adobe sites with 65K SKUs each is a pre-Kontoor architecture. We help brands rationalize their digital estate ahead of group integration milestones."
**Named buyer:** Joumana Lovstad (CMO) confirmed. CDO needed — research via Kontoor/Helly Hansen leadership page.
**Action:** Draft outreach to Joumana Lovstad. Frame around Investor Day September 2 as a deadline — "before Oslo, not after."
**Estimated deal:** €300–500K (rationalization scope dependent)
**Window:** ~24 weeks to Investor Day. Act in April to be in a diagnostic by June.
